message:
bd-npn8e: Deterministic observability tests

Cross-component tests for evidence ledger determinism, e-process alarm
behavior, and conformal coverage diagnostics:

- evidence_ledger_determinism_mixed_outcomes: same mixed outcome sequence
  on independent supervisors yields identical ledger entries
- storm_monitor_intensity_window_integration: window+monitor pair
  produces deterministic e-values across runs
- eprocess_alert_transitions_monotone: watching precedes alert
- supervisor_storm_combined_determinism: supervisor+window+monitor
  combined produce identical decisions, e-values, and intensities
- evidence_ledger_binding_constraints_cover_all_paths: exercises
  RestartAllowed, WindowExhausted, and MonotoneSeverity variants
- conformal_coverage_guarantee_holds: empirical coverage ≥ 1-α
- health_threshold_coverage_guarantee_holds: same for health metrics
- conformal_anomaly_detection_deterministic: same sequence → same result
- conformal_report_well_calibrated_diagnostics: report text/JSON coverage

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-20to","title":"[ALIEN][RAPTORQ] Proof-carrying decode trace artifact (explainable failures)","description":"## Purpose

When decoding succeeds or fails, produce a compact artifact that explains why:
- which equations were used
- which pivots chosen
- which symbols were missing

This supports:
- deterministic debugging
- replay
- auditability ("why did we accept/reject?")

## Acceptance criteria

- Artifact is deterministic and bounded in size.
- A failing decode produces an explainable reason with enough context to reproduce.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:12:46.778414645Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:08:31.674086548Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T05:08:31.674012650Z","close_reason":"Implemented proof artifact system: DecodeProof, DecodeConfig, ReceivedSummary, PeelingTrace, EliminationTrace, ProofOutcome types with builder pattern and deterministic hashing. 4 passing tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","raptorq","trace","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20to","depends_on_id":"bd-201j","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-20to","depends_on_id":"bd-s8c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-20way","title":"Canonical lock order + guard strategy","description":"Define and document a deterministic lock acquisition order for all RuntimeState shards.

Scope:
- Establish a global lock order (e.g., Regions → Tasks → Obligations → Futures → Time).
- Specify allowed lock combinations and disallowed cycles.
- Define lightweight guard helpers or utilities to enforce ordering in code.

Acceptance:
- Written lock order spec in issue text (usable without external docs).
- Guidance for refactors in worker/scheduler/local_queue paths.
- Notes on deadlock avoidance and how to extend the order for new shards.","notes":"Lock order spec added to docs/runtime_state_contention_inventory.md. Order: E(Config) → D(Instrumentation) → B(Regions) → A(Tasks) → C(Obligations). Includes lock combination table for all operations, disallowed sequences, proposed ShardGuard API, and guidance for extending the order. Unblocks bd-2wfti, bd-1b7sk, bd-1rhpr, bd-3hzrf.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FuchsiaCliff","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:55:23.855879534Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T20:08:22.211901730Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T20:06:46.508388618Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20way","depends_on_id":"bd-23kbc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:36.730548581Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-20way","depends_on_id":"bd-2ijqf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:55:23.885787732Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":177,"issue_id":"bd-20way","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Initial lock-order sketch (to refine after inventory):
1) Regions shard
2) Tasks shard
3) Obligations shard
4) Futures/StoredTask shard
5) Time/Clock shard
6) Observability/Trace/Metrics shard (read-mostly; ideally lock-free)

Rules of thumb:
- Acquire locks only as long as needed; prefer scoped guard helpers.
- Avoid nested locking when a single shard will do; pass minimal data across shards.
- Cross-entity ops (cancel propagation, region close, obligation resolution) must follow this order strictly.
- If new shards appear later, insert them into a single global order and update helpers/tests.","created_at":"2026-02-04T20:08:22Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-212q","title":"[ALIEN][RAPTORQ][ENC] Robust soliton degree generator (deterministic)","description":"## Purpose

Implement the degree distribution used for repair symbols:
- robust soliton (or RFC-aligned alternative)
- deterministic sampling from explicit seed

## Acceptance criteria

- Sampling is deterministic.
- Distribution parameters are configurable and validated.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:15:36.568722613Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:34:21.308199437Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T04:34:21.308126893Z","close_reason":"RobustSoliton implemented in src/raptorq/systematic.rs with configurable c/delta params, deterministic sampling, CDF-based binary search, parameter validation, and 9 comprehensive tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","encoding","math","raptorq"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-212q","depends_on_id":"bd-2kr1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T01:54:23Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-21f9","title":"E2E: Runtime Lifecycle & Structured Concurrency Tests","description":"Comprehensive E2E tests for runtime lifecycle and structured concurrency. Scenarios: nested region create/teardown, task spawn/join across regions, cancellation propagation through region tree, obligation tracking, budget enforcement, panic recovery, quiescence after complex workloads, scheduler fairness. Lab runtime for deterministic variants. Log region tree state at phase transitions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:13:50.752889674Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T18:58:39.099983948Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T18:58:39.099913998Z","close_reason":"Added 8 comprehensive E2E tests: nested region lifecycle, cancellation propagation, obligation lifecycle/abort, budget poll quota enforcement, complex workload quiescence, deterministic nested regions, report aggregation. All pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","runtime","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-21f9","depends_on_id":"bd-1dgr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:06Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":89,"issue_id":"bd-21f9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Every test function must: (1) call init_test_logging() at start; (2) use test_phase!("test_name") for the top-level test marker; (3) use test_section!("phase") for each distinct phase (setup, execution, verification, teardown); (4) use assert_with_log!() instead of bare assert!() for all assertions that compare values; (5) call test_complete!("test_name") on success; (6) log key intermediate state (e.g., connection count, region tree depth, bytes transferred) at INFO level with tracing::info!() so failures can be diagnosed from log output alone.","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:29:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-21k66","title":"[SPORK][App][Lab] AppScenario Integration (seed+config -> report)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T05:43:30.760726922Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T05:45:08.213481061Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["app","dpor","lab","spork"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-21k66","depends_on_id":"bd-1vk6c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:36:12Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-21k66","depends_on_id":"bd-1zkki","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:36:12Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-21k66","depends_on_id":"bd-8dxy1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:36:12Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-21k66","depends_on_id":"bd-sd083","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:36:12Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":272,"issue_id":"bd-21k66","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Goal

Make "run my app under lab" a one-liner.

This is a usability requirement for coding agents: the system should produce deterministic reports and crash artifacts by default.

## Deliverable

- `AppScenario` (or similar) that:
  - takes `AppSpec` + seed/config
  - runs under `LabRuntime`
  - returns a `LabRunReport` (fingerprint + oracle results)
  - optionally attaches crashpack builder

## Acceptance

- Given same seed+spec, report fingerprint is stable.
- Scenario runner can be used by DPOR explorer without bespoke glue.
- Failures automatically include crashpack metadata.
","created_at":"2026-02-06T05:45:08Z"}]}
